description_long: This bungalow features a prominent, steeply pitched hip roof covered in reddish-brown shingles, extending well beyond the main structure to provide ample shade. The front facade is defined by a raised porch with decorative wooden railings and balusters, accessed by a short flight of steps. The structure is elevated on sturdy supports, suggesting protection from ground moisture or flooding. The exterior walls are finished in a warm, natural wood tone, and the porch area is open, inviting entry. The side and rear views reveal consistent railing designs and a lack of visible windows, emphasizing the porch as the main architectural feature. The overall design is compact, single-story, and evokes a traditional Southeast Asian or tropical bungalow style.
description: A compact, single-story bungalow with a steep hip roof, raised wooden porch, and decorative railings, finished in warm natural tones.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, raised porch with steps, and decorative railings under a wide hip roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended roof eaves and continuous porch railing, with no visible windows.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall and supporting structure, with the hip roof and porch railing visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring the other side, showing the extended roof and porch railing.
